data.body:
G3 container coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child fe57 "Hermetic-but-honest Jira fixtures + verified fake client (served through the production path)"), re-cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57).

This child is the FOUNDATION story and FULLY covers its owned slice; no per-criterion coverage gap is attributable to fe57 itself:
- Parent AC2 (real-payload-fixtures half) → fe57 AC1 (tests/fixtures/jira/*.json scrubbed real payloads for search+comment+issuelinks+parent; no tokens/emails/accountIds) + AC2 (FakeAcliClient serves them through the production fetch_snapshot enrichment, NO shape transformation) + AC4 (consumed only via production path, no hand-reshape). Round-trip contract test correctly delegated OUT to sibling 8937 (depends_on fe57). FULLY contributes the fixture half.
- Parent SC1 (no silent producer/consumer shape drift) → fe57 supplies hermetic-but-honest fixtures + verified fake flowing through production fetch_snapshot, eliminating the bug-0ee6 root cause; reinforced by 8937 (joint round-trip) and 59ef (verified-fake fidelity). FULLY contributes the foundation half.
- Parent AC1 (SSOT schema) → fe57 feeds the producer path 8937's schema pins; artifact owned by 8937.

Criteria not owned are correctly delegated to named siblings: AC1 schema + AC2 round-trip test → 8937; AC3/SC2 → 33d0; AC4/SC3 → 2359; AC5/SC4 → 822a; verified-fake honesty/dual-run → 59ef. fe57's declared OUT items (contract test→8937; honesty check→59ef) are consistent with the roster.

4-bucket for this pairing: AC2 (fixtures half) + SC1 (foundation half) FULLY by THIS child; AC1 contributed (artifact→8937); AC2 round-trip/AC3/AC4/AC5/SC2/SC3/SC4 covered by named siblings; SC5/AC7 anti-change-detector posture applied per-test; AC7 structural epic roll-up; AC6 PARTIALLY covered (single roster-level absence below).

ONE ROSTER-LEVEL absence (NOT this child's fault; already tracked on siblings 2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937 — restated for this pairing's record after re-cross-checking the WHOLE roster INCLUDING this child, and confirmed by repo search: docs/mutation-testing.md is a general mutation-testing doc, not the AC6 governing artifact, and no child references producing/extending a reconciler anti-change-detector guardrail):
- finding: Parent AC6 ("A documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNS new tests — no mock-call-count, no whole-blob golden equality; assert semantic round-trip + structure/type") is only PARTIALLY covered. Every child, including fe57, only APPLIES the anti-change-detector posture to its OWN deliverable; no sibling owns a deliverable that PRODUCES a documented governing guardrail/policy artifact that governs FUTURE/new reconciler tests (AC6's operative verb is "governs new tests").
  - criteria: parent AC6
  - location: parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 Acceptance Criteria bullet 6; cross-checked against siblings 2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57
  - evidence: child fe57 references the concern only as "a documented re-capture procedure so fixtures stay honest and do not rot into change-detectors" — self-scoped to its own fixtures, not a governing guardrail for new reconciler tests; siblings 2359 AC4, 33d0 AC4, 822a AC4, 8937 AC4, 59ef AC3 are likewise self-scoped; repo search returned no existing reconciler anti-change-detector governing artifact (only general docs/mutation-testing.md).
  - scenarios: a new reconciler test added next cycle has no documented guardrail to conform to; the anti-change-detector intent silently erodes once these stories close
  - impact: parent AC6 cannot be fully checked off by closing the current children as written (4-bucket: PARTIALLY covered — posture applied per-test, documented governing artifact absent)
  - checklist_item: union of children covers each parent acceptance/success criterion
  - suggested_fix: assign the documented-guardrail deliverable to one story (natural home repeatedly named across the roster: keystone 8937 — e.g. a new AC "a documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests") or add a small dedicated child, so AC6's documented governing artifact has an owner.

No coverage finding is attributable to child fe57 itself for its owned criteria (fixtures/fake foundation fully covered); the single absence above is a roster-level AC6 gap already tracked across the sibling set.
